entries.prompt_text: Replace {leverage} with {use} in the {recommendation paragraph}

Replace {optimize} with {improve} in the {timeline note}
Replace {synergy} with {collaboration} in the {strategy section}

Delete {At the end of the day} from the {closing sentence}
Delete {Moving forward} from the first sentence under {Next Steps}
Delete {It should be noted that} from {footnote 2}
Delete {In order to} from the first paragraph under {Findings}
Delete {Needless to say} from the {summary box}
Delete {As previously mentioned} from {paragraph 6}
Delete {It goes without saying that} from the {introduction}

Delete {At this point in time} from the {status update}
Delete {Due to the fact that} from the {rationale paragraph} and replace with {Because}

Change {we'll} to {we will} in the {opening paragraph}
Change {doesn't} to {does not} in the {closing paragraph}
Change {The review was completed by the team} to {The team completed the review} in {paragraph 2}
Change {The proposal is being reviewed} to {The committee is reviewing the proposal} in the {summary note}
Replace {I think} in the {recommendation paragraph} with {The data shows}
Change {we've} to {we have} in the {final paragraph}
Change {can't} to {cannot} in the {limitations section}
Change {The budget was approved by finance} to {Finance approved the budget} in the {status section}
Change {won't} to {will not} in the {disclaimer}
Change {The data was analyzed by the research team} to {The research team analyzed the data} in {paragraph 5}
Change {it's} to {it is} in the {executive summary}
Change {Errors were found in the report} to {The audit found errors in the report} in the {findings section}
Change {shouldn't} to {should not} in the {policy note}
Change {The contract was signed by both parties} to {Both parties signed the contract} in the {closing section}
Replace {you guys} with {the team} in the {meeting notes} section
Split the run-on sentence in the {closing paragraph} into two sentences
Break the long sentence under {Risks} at the semicolon into two sentences
Split the second sentence in {paragraph 3} into two shorter sentences
Break the comma splice in the {opening paragraph} into two sentences
Split the sentence in {footnote 1} after the first clause
Break the {six-line paragraph} under {Results} into two paragraphs at the topic shift
Split the {40-word sentence} in the {methodology section} into two shorter sentences
Break the sentence in the {summary callout} that has {three dependent clauses} into separate sentences
Split the compound sentence in {paragraph 1} at {and} into two sentences
Break the long list sentence in the {scope section} into a bulleted list
Split the run-on in the {recommendation section} where two ideas are joined by a comma
Break the {five-clause sentence} in the {findings section} into three shorter sentences
Split the dense {opening sentence} of the {executive summary} into two sentences
Break the {parenthetical-heavy sentence} in {paragraph 4} into a main sentence and a follow-up
Split the sentence in the {conclusion} that restates three findings into one sentence per finding
Add a comma after {However} in {paragraph 4}
Add a comma before {but} in the second sentence of {paragraph 3}
Change {companys} to {company's} in the {sidebar}
Change {neither option are} to {neither option is} in the {recommendation paragraph}
Change {is implementing} to {implemented} in the {recap paragraph} to fix the tense
Add a period where the run-on starts in {paragraph 5}
Change {recomendation} to {recommendation} in the {heading}
Add an apostrophe to {clients} in the phrase {clients needs} in {paragraph 3}
Change {their} to {there} in the {opening sentence} where it refers to a location
Change {effect} to {affect} in the {findings section} where it is used as a verb
Fix the subject-verb agreement by changing {the data show} to {the data shows} in {paragraph 2}
Change {alot} to {a lot} in the {summary paragraph}
